evidence_quotes.quote: Google's announced plans to further restrict or eliminate the ability to sideload applications on Android devices in the near future.
The practice of sideloadinginstalling applications from sources other than the official Google Play Storehas been a cornerstone of Android's open ecosystem since its inception.

paragraphs.text: Dear Members of the European Commission and Parliament, I am writing to express my serious concern regarding Google's announced plans to further restrict or eliminate the ability to sideload applications on Android devices in the near future. As a European citizen and technology user, I believe these changes would fundamentally undermine digital freedom, consumer choice, and fair competition in the mobile app ecosystem. The practice of sideloadinginstalling applications from sources other than the official Google Play Storehas been a cornerstone of Android's open ecosystem since its inception. This capability allows users to: - Install applications from alternative app stores and independent developers - Access software that may not be available through Google's official channels - Maintain control over their own devices and digital autonomy - Support competition and innovation in the mobile app market - Exercise their fundamental right to choose how they use their purchased hardware Google's proposed restrictions would create a dangerous precedent that contradicts the spirit and letter of the Digital Markets Act (DMA), which explicitly requires gatekeepers to allow users to install applications from third-party sources. By eliminating sideloading capabilities, Google would establish an effective monopoly over software distribution on Android devices, stifling innovation and harming consumers. Furthermore, these restrictions would disproportionately impact: - Small and independent developers who rely on alternative distribution methods - Users in regions with limited access to official app stores - Privacy-conscious consumers who prefer open-source alternatives - Enterprises and organizations requiring specialized software solutions I urge the European Union to take immediate action to prevent Google from implementing these anti-competitive measures. The Commission should: 1. Investigate these planned restrictions under existing antitrust regulations 2. Clarify that eliminating sideloading capabilities violates the DMA requirements 3. Impose appropriate sanctions if Google proceeds with these limitations 4. Strengthen regulations to ensure long-term protection of user choice and market competition The European Union has consistently championed digital rights and fair competition. I trust that you will act decisively to protect European consumers and maintain the principles of digital freedom that are essential to our democratic values. Thank you for your attention to this critical matter. I look forward to your swift action to preserve consumer choice and prevent the creation of a mobile app monopoly. Sincerely,
